lesson.menu_desc: Block 3 review: polite requests, used to, present perfect continuous, first conditional + summarizing vocabulary -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Review -- My World in English -- Bringing Block 3 Together
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sandra Hayasaki | Aula 15 -- Review -- My World in English

listenings.text: Let me summarize what these last weeks have meant to me. Overall, I feel more confident speaking English about my real life. In general, I used to avoid long conversations, but now I handle them much better. The biggest challenge for me was talking about the past: how I used to do things, and what I have been doing lately. Now it feels more natural. Of course, I still make mistakes, and sometimes I need to clear up a misunderstanding. But on the whole, things go smoothly now. If I keep practicing every day, I will keep improving. When I look back at where I started, I am proud. Learning a language is not one big jump; it is many small steps that, over time, add up to a real change.

I want to summarize my own journey in this block. Overall, I am surprised by how much I can say now. In general, I used to give one-word answers, but now I can tell a whole story. I have been practicing every day, and it shows. The biggest challenge for me was technology vocabulary, but I handle it better now. Some grammar was hard at first, especially the conditional, until my teacher helped me clear up my doubts. On the whole, my classes go smoothly, and I enjoy them. If something is difficult, I no longer panic; I ask a question and try again. When I think about where I began, I feel proud. I am 51, and I am learning a new language. As long as I keep going, my world in English will keep growing.

extra_audio.text: First, say the topic in one line, so your listener knows what you will talk about. Then, in general, give the main idea before the details. Next, mention one challenge you had and how you handle it now. After that, clear up anything that might be confusing, with a short example. Finally, on the whole, give your overall feeling, so the summary ends in a clear and positive way.
